name: Two Hour Nut Rolls- Quick Potica
recipeInstructions:
Source: Treasured Slovenian & International Recipes
DIRECTIONS Dissolve yeast in warm milk. Stir until the mixture is smooth.
Combine flour, sugar, and salt in a separate bowl. Add warm evaporated milk, melted butter, and beaten egg to this dry mixture.
Gradually add the yeast mixture while stirring continuously.
Divide dough into two equal parts. Roll each part out as thin as possible and spread with honey nut filling. Prepare the filling before making the dough to ensure it is ready for immediate spreading.
DIRECTIONS For the filling:
1 lb. Nut meats, ground
1/2 cup Butter or margarine, melted
1/2 cup Warm Evaporated Milk
1/2 cup Honey
1 Egg, beaten well
1 cup Sugar
2 cups Yellow Raisins (Sultanas)
1 tsp Cinnamon
Mix scalded milk with nuts. Add sugar, honey, and melted butter; stir until combined.
When cool, add the beaten egg and cinnamon.
Spread this mixture over the rolled dough and sprinkle raisins on top.
Roll up as for a jelly roll and place in a warm area to rise until doubled in size.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 35 - 40 minutes. Before serving, brush the top with an beaten egg mixed with half a cup of milk for a golden crust.
VARIATIONS
Grandma uses pecans or English walnuts and excludes raisins. She rolls out the dough on a smooth towel to make it easier to roll up. Cover the potica while rising with the same towel.
